decisions.decision: Use the reconstructed task-local question set as the auditable driver for this leaf and keep the scope tied to the parent step-4 preservation rules instead of inventing broader backend work.
alternatives_rejected: Treat the missing local question file as permission to redefine the task scope.
Plan speculative backend or test expansion work not anchored to the validation artifact.

decisions.decision: Treat the preserved `BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests` slice as the execution gate for any `backend/server.py` change. Because the focused sentinel currently passes locally, the default implementation path is no production-code edit.
alternatives_rejected: Patch the protected-write seam despite the preserved sentinel remaining green.
Broaden the failure signal to unrelated routes or unpreserved tests before deciding whether the shared seam regressed.

decisions.decision: If execution later reproduces a preserved-sentinel failure, limit the fix to the shared protected-write seam in `backend/server.py` and avoid route-local special cases; otherwise keep this leaf as a no-op outside `.fleet/tasks/`.
alternatives_rejected: Introduce route-specific auth or limiter branches to satisfy one endpoint.
Continue touching production code after a clean sentinel audit.

steps.task: Reconfirm the parent gate by running the preserved `BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests` sentinel and mapping its missing-agent, malformed-agent, duplicate-header, quota-header, `429`, and `Retry-After` assertions back to the shared protected-write seam in `backend/server.py`.
If the preserved sentinel remains green, make no changes to `backend/server.py` or route-local handlers and keep the branch scoped to task artifacts only for this leaf.

Only if step 1 exposes a real regression, patch the central protected-write path at `resolve_write_request_agent(...)`, `_enforce_write_controls()`, `rate_limit_headers(...)`, or `BayesHandler.handle_api()` so the existing negative-auth and rate-limit contracts pass again without endpoint-specific special cases.

Re-run the focused auth/rate-limit sentinel after either the no-op decision or any shared-seam fix, then inspect the diff to verify the outcome stays inside `.fleet/tasks/` unless a reproduced shared-seam defect required the backend edit.
Stage the resulting task artifact changes and commit them with the requested message once the scoped outcome is verified.
